content: The orthographic variant relationship between 'Reflexion' (the older, non-standard spelling of reflection in English language education) and the standard term 'reflection' can be borrowed to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms: building a dual spelling/framing memory bank that stores the same problem-solving reasoning in two linguistically distinct forms, one as the original output and one as re-framed 'reflexion' variant. This dual framing creates natural inconsistent solving perspectives that trigger more robust critical self-evaluation.
